results.title: Viatris AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Viatris Inc. is a global pharmaceutical company formed in 2020 through the merger of Mylan and Pfizer's Upjohn division, positioned as a bridge between generics and brands [1]. While the company has not prominently featured AI in its public communications or earnings calls, it has begun exploring AI-driven collaborations and digital transformation initiatives [2]. In October 2025, Viatris partnered with Locus Biosciences to develop AI-powered bacteriophage therapies for ophthalmic infections, marking its first significant AI-focused initiative [3]. The company has also pursued digital transformation, particularly during the COVID-19 pandemic, under CIO Ramkumar Rayapureddy's leadership [4]. However, Viatris appears to be in the early stages of AI adoption compared to other pharmaceutical companies, with most initiatives focused on operational efficiency rather than transformative AI applications.

results.title: Lexie Mills
highlights:
Senior Manager Business Intelligence and Analytics, Commercial Performance and Insights at [Viatris](https://www.linkedin.com/company/viatris)
...
My experience for the last six years has been with Mylan Pharmaceuticals in Morgantown WV. I started as an intern in IT and now work as a Manager in NA Business Intelligence and Analytics. I have a passion to work hard and inspire others to do the same and this has propelled me through my career. Over the years I have managed 5 of the top 7 pharmacy accounts, and been responsible for their daily needs and financial analysis. My team is currently responsible for product management in North America and making recommendations to leadership for each product family. Our evaluation includes product launches, growth, discontinuations and price changes. I am also very active with my community, specifically in the agriculture community. One example is my involvement with Pennsylvania Club Livestock Association (PCLA). I am the president and at large direct for this organization, where we sanction multiple small animal shows in Pennsylvania for youth 0-21. We have over 200 youth participating at each show, for grand prizes at the end of the year. I am also the director of Fayette County Premier Livestock Show. This livestock show exhibits over 700 animals and 250 participants. I am very hard working individual and I love challenges that push my boundaries and teach me new things. I have an outgoing personality and enjoy working with the community, collaborating with my staff and mentoring individuals around me.
...

...
Jan 2012 - Present (14 years and 5 months)
...
Viatris (VTRS) is a Pharmaceutical Manufacturing company. At Viatris, we empower people worldwide to live healthier at every stage of life. Viatris employs 12,964 people (+3.6% YoY, +623 people) and has an annual revenue of $14.3B, founded in 2020. Headquartered in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, United States, operates in 98 countries (including India, Ireland, France, Italy, and United Kingdom). Has a $13.5B market cap. IPO 2020.

Hi, I’m Shrusti Adlak, a Computer Science Engineering (AI/ML) undergrad with a passion for creating AI-powered solutions that positively impact lives. With hands-on experience in tools like GPT-3, Azure Machine Learning, and Roberta, I specialize in blending emotional intelligence with artificial intelligence. Whether it’s developing a mental health chatbot using GPT-3 and Azure, forecasting sales with Azure ML Studio, or creating a digital diary with emotion detection—my projects are guided by one principle: measurable impact. I hold Microsoft certifications (DP-100 & DP-900) that validate my cloud and data expertise, and I’ve complemented my tech skills with soft skills training in public speaking, teamwork, and leadership. What sets me apart? A balance of technical precision and human-centered thinking. I don’t just build systems; I build solutions that connect. # Principal Data Scientist, Data and AI Convergence

Employer AbbVie Location North Chicago, IL Salary 141500 - 268500 USD Start date Jun 16, 2026

The Principal Data Scientist will serve as a strategic leader and technical expert driving enterprise-level transformation through the convergence of R&D data assets and the systematic integration of advanced AI/ML capabilities into organizational workflows. This role will work with cross-functional teams to define and architect scalable solutions that fundamentally transform how AbbVie leverages data and artificial intelligence to enhance strategic decision-making processes across the R&D pipeline and clinical development continuum.

Acting as a key member of the R&D Convergence Core team, this position will identify systemic gaps in data convergence and workflow integration, then lead collaborative efforts to design and implement enterprise-wide solutions that create sustainable organizational capabilities rather than isolated point solutions. The Senior Principal Data Scientist will work at the intersection of scientific strategy, advanced analytics, and organizational transformation to establish integrated frameworks that enable scalable AI/ML adoption across therapeutic areas and functional domains.

results.title: Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3055061
highlights:
Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery job with Eli Lilly and Company | 3055061
...
# Scientific Lead, Generative AI Engineer, Applied Intelligence for Discovery
...
Employer Eli Lilly and Company Location San Francisco, California Start date Jun 15, 2026
...
## Job Details
...
We are building something unprecedented, an AI foundation that will fundamentally change how drug discovery research is conducted.
...
The Applied Intelligence for Discovery (AI4D) team is a newly formed group within Lilly Research Laboratories that operates at the intersection of scientific delivery and core platform development. AI4D’s mission is to connecting scientists to petabyte-scale data through natural language interfaces, automated analysis workflows, and intelligent search — and to convert early deployments into repeatable system standards and evaluation practices that scale across therapeutic areas.
...
As a Generative AI Engineer, you will design, build, and operate the core AI systems that power this transformation: retrieval-augmented generation over internal scientific documents, text-to-SQL over complex omics databases, agentic workflows that automate multi-step analyses, and the evaluation infrastructure that able the next-generation of medicines for patients.
...
Key Responsibilities
...
Design, build, and optimize RAG pipelines over internal publications, study reports, electronic lab notebooks, and other scientific documents
...
Build hybrid retrieval systems combining vector search with structured metadata, knowledge graphs, and ontology-aware filtering
...
Build and optimize text-to-SQL systems over Lilly’s databases, enabling scientists to query gene expression, proteomics, pathway, and variant data through natural language
...
Develop schema documentation, semantic annotations, and gold-standard question/SQL pairs that bridge how scientists think about data and how it is stored
...
Implement multi-step reasoning approaches (chain-of-thought, self-correction, Reflexion loops) to improve accuracy on complex scientific queries
...
Design agentic AI workflows that chain database queries, bioinformatics tools, literature search, and visualization into automated multi-step scientific analyses
...
Evaluate and integrate emerging orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, custom architectures) for scientific use cases
...
Build evaluation frameworks measuring accuracy, reliability, and scientific validity of AI outputs
...
PhD in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related technical field with 0-3+ years of experience; or equivalent experience building production LLM systems; MS in Computer Science, Data Science, or a related technical field with 5+ years of experience; or equivalent experience building production LLM systems
...
Experience building LLM-powered applications, including at least two of: RAG systems, text-to-SQL, agentic workflows, or fine-tuning pipelines
...
Strong software engineering skills in Python with experience building production-grade systems
...
Deep familiarity with the modern LLM ecosystem: embedding models, vector databases, and orchestration frameworks
...
Experience designing evaluation frameworks for LLM systems — systematic approaches to measuring accuracy, detecting hallucinations, and tracking regressions
...
Comfort working with complex, heterogeneous data — databases with hundreds of tables, specialized schemas, or domain-specific vocabularies
...
Familiarity with
...
computing environments (
...
Familiarity with biomedical data types (omics, clinical, molecular) or scientific databases
...
Experience with MLOps/LLMOps tooling: experiment tracking, model registries, prompt versioning, A/B testing for AI systems
...
Knowledge of biomedical ontologies (Gene Ontology, MeSH, ChEBI) or experience integrating domain-specific knowledge into LLM systems

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
...
# Data Scientist
...
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
...
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
...
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
...

- Applies advanced techniques including statistics, machine learning, simulation, and mathematical modelling
...
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
...
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
...
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into clear, actionable insights for business stakeholders, product teams, and leadership to support data driven decision making
...
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities

highlights:
Healthcare Data Scientist & AI Solutions - Xtalks

## Healthcare Data Scientist & AI Solutions
...
### Job Description
...
BioVid is transforming pharmaceutical market research by replacing slow, traditional methodologies with AI-augmented data systems.In this role, you will personally analyze the data to uncover HCP prescribing behavior and behavioral insights. You will help bridge quantitative real-world data with qualitative market research, and play a key role in developing synthetic audiences ("Digital Twins") that mirror healthcare provider prescribing behavior and patient treatment journeys, while enabling scalable forecasting, segmentation, and market intelligence solutions.Must-Have QualificationsHands-on analysis of medical / pharmacy claims data (EHR preferred)Direct, hands-on experience analyzing the data itself - not only engineering it. You can extract and predict HCP prescribing behavior for specific drugs, for specific conditions, in specific treatment areas, and tag HCP qualitative / attitudinal segments to NPIs, associating those segments with real prescribing behavior. HIPAA requirements fluent (most of our data will be deidentified)
...
- Contact
- Analyze medical and pharmacy claims to extract and predict HCP prescribing behavior for specific drugs, conditions, and treatment areas.
- Perform HCP / patient segmentation, demand forecasting, journey mapping, modeling
- Help build Synthetics, simulated segments and HCP equivalents.
- Machine Learning experience in modeling training and evaluating models (LLM fine tuning, training a nice to have)
- Tag qualitative / attitudinal HCP segments to NPIs and link them to observed prescribing behavior.
...
- Translate these analyses into actionable segmentation, targeting, and forecasting insights.
...
Analytics, AI & Machine Learning
...
- Conduct advanced exploratory data analysis (EDA) and feature engineering using SQL, Python, pandas, and numpy.
- Analyze claims data to model and predict HCP prescribing behavior by drug, condition, and treatment area, and associate attitudinal segments with NPIs.
- Build AI-powered synthetic provider and patient personas grounded in real-world claims distributions.
- Develop methods to scale qualitative research insights into national quantitative projections.
- Machine Learning experience in modeling training and evaluating models. (LLM fine tuning and training a nice to have

- We are seeking a highly skilled Senior Data Scientist to join our ML Digital Experience & Digital Workplace team. In this role, you will lead the design, development, and deployment of advanced machine learning models and Generative AI (GenAI) solutions that transform the digital workplace experience. You will work at the intersection of data science, AI innovation, and enterprise technology to deliver intelligent products and capabilities that drive productivity, automation, and enhanced user experiences across the organization. This is an opportunity to shape the future of how employees interact with digital tools and platforms by leveraging cutting-edge ML and GenAI technologies to solve complex business challenges at scale.
...
- Machine Learning & AI Development: Design, build, and deploy production-grade ML models with end-to-end pipelines and MLOps best practices to ensure scalability, reproducibility, and continuous improvement.
...
Generative AI & LLMs: Lead the design and deployment of GenAI solutions leveraging LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, and fine-tuning to build intelligent assistants, conversational agents, and knowledge retrieval tools.
...
Data Analysis & Insights: Analyze large-scale datasets using statistical methods and advanced analytical frameworks to uncover actionable patterns and measure AI/ML impact.
...
Digital Workplace & Employee Experience: Partner cross-functionally to deliver high-impact AI/ML use cases—including predictive analytics, anomaly detection, and workflow automation—across enterprise digital workplace platforms.

Collaboration, Technical Leadership & AI Governance: Serve as a subject matter expert in ML and GenAI, mentoring team members, driving actionable business recommendations, and contributing to responsible AI governance frameworks.

Experience with computer vision or multi-modal AI, prior work in healthcare or enterprise IT environments, and relevant cloud AI/ML certifications (Azure AI Engineer, AWS ML Specialty, Google Professional ML Engineer).

results.title: LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
highlights:
LLM Engineer job with VIB | 12860950
...
# LLM Engineer
...
Employer VIB Location Leuven Closing date 19 Aug 2026
...
VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact.
...
The VIB Center for Neuroscience Leuven (CNL) investigates the mechanisms of brain function and neurological disease across the scales of biology, spanning neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment, synaptic biology, and computational neuroscience. AI and large language models have transformative potential across these research areas.
...
We are now looking for an LLM Engineer in a shared position between VIB.AI's Machine Learning Expertise Unit and the Center for Neuroscience. You will split your time between building institute-wide AI tools and applying them directly to neuroscience research challenges.
...
As an LLM Engineer in this shared position, you will:
...
- Develop and deploy LLM-based pilot projects tailored to neuroscience research at the CNL, applying AI to questions in molecular, cellular and circuit neuroscience, neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment and disease mechanisms.
...
- Build and maintain components of VIB's institutional intelligence layer, including agentic bioinformatics workflows, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, and AI-assisted experimental design tools.
...
- Design, implement, and evaluate LLM-based solutions for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.
...
- Collaborate with wet-lab and computational neuroscience researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI and LLMs can accelerate scientific progress.
...
- Work with the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and VIB's AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools that serve the broader VIB community.
...
- Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of LLMs in line with best practices and international standards.
...
- Mine opportunities and data at the center’s advanced technology expertise centers and affiliated central core facilities.
...
- Leverage VIB's Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale LLM development and deployment.
...
- PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, computational neuroscience, statistics, or a related field.
- Strong experience with large language models, including pretraining, fine-tuning, prompt engineering, and evaluation.
- Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).
- Experience with real-world applications of LLMs in scientific or biomedical contexts is a plus.
...
- Familiarity with neuroscience data types (imaging, electrophysiology, genomics, proteomics, or clinical data) is a plus but not required.
...
- Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.
- A strong interest in applying LLMs to impactful life science and neuroscience problems.
...
- The opportunity to pioneer applications of LLMs in neuroscience and the broader life sciences.
...
- Direct access to VIB's Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.
...
Please complete the application through the VIB online application tool. A complete application file should contain the following documents:
...
For further information, please send an email to stein.aerts@kuleuven.be or joost.schymkowitz@kuleuven.be with subject title Inquiry: LLM Engineer CNL/VIB.AI.

results.title: Machine Learning (ML) Engineer at VIB.AI job with VIB | 12860981
highlights:
Machine Learning (ML) Engineer at VIB.AI job with VIB | 12860981
...
# Machine Learning (ML) Engineer at VIB.AI
...
Employer VIB Location Leuven Closing date 21 Aug 2026
...
## Job Details
...
Application deadline: August 31, 2026 About us VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact. We are now looking for a Machine Learning (ML) Engineer to join the Machine Learning Expertise Unit at VIB.AI. Your role As an ML Engineer, you will: Develop and deploy ML solutions in close collaboration with researchers across VIB centers, applying state-of-the-art methods to cutting-edge biomedical questions.Design, implement, and evaluate ML and foundation-model approaches, including LLM-based solutions, for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.Collaborate with researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI can accelerate scientific progress.Work within the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and with VIB AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools.Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of AI in line with best practices and international standards.Leverage VIB Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale ML development and deployment. Your profile PhD in machine learning, computer science, computational biology, statistics, or a related field.Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).Experience developing AI applications for scientific or biomedical research, including LLM-based and agentic AI systems, is a plus.Ability to work collaboratively across disciplines and communicate effectively with researchers from different backgrounds.A strong interest in applying ML to impactful life science problems.We welcome applications from both early-career and experienced ML engineers. Our offer A stimulating, international, interdisciplinary, collaborative, and supportive work environment, which emphasizes diversity and inclusion.An environment that promotes personal development.The opportunity to pioneer applications of ML in biomedical sciences.Direct access to VIB’s Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.A competitive salary and benefits package, with relocation support if needed.The chance to make a lasting impact on science, health, and society. About VIB.AI VIB.AI is the artificial intelligence initiative of VIB, designed to accelerate breakthroughs in life sciences by integrating advanced AI methods into research, discovery, and innovation. For more information, visit vib.ai/en. How to apply Please complete the application through the VIB online application tool (https://jobs.vib.be/apply/135914). A complete application file should contain the following documents: Your motivation letter stating career goals, past experience, and how these relate to the current position.Your CV.Contact information of three references. Application deadline: August 31st 2026. For further information, please send an email to pedro.goncalves@kuleuven.be, dmitry.kobak@ugent.be or stein.aerts@kuleuven.be with subject title Inquiry: ML Engineer.

highlights:

...
# Machine Learning Scientist
...
Strativ Group• United State
...
Lead the design, development, and training of large, generative deep learning systems for small molecule drug discovery. Develop and maintain training pipelines, evaluate model quality, and collaborate with colleagues. Strong Python and PyTorch experience required.
...
Design and implement improvements to large co-folding model architectures
...
Build and maintain training pipelines that support reliable, rapid, and large-scale experimentation
...
Python PyTorch Deep Learning
...
### Job Description
...
A well-funded, AI-native biotech on a mission to deliver better medicines through innovation in AI-based drug discovery. The company has developed proprietary deep learning technology for protein-ligand co-folding and structure prediction, and is now scaling that technology to power the next generation of small molecule drug discovery programmes. This is a high-impact scientific role sitting at the intersection of cutting-edge ML research and real-world therapeutic application.
...
We are looking for a Machine Learning Scientist to lead the design, development, training, and benchmarking of the next version of our co-folding model - a large, generative deep learning system that predicts how small molecules bind to protein targets. You will own the full model development lifecycle, from architecture design through to training at scale and deployment-ready evaluation.
...
Key Responsibilities
...
- Design and implement improvements to large co-folding model architectures and training objectives for small molecule drug discovery
- Build and maintain training pipelines that support reliable, rapid, and large-scale experimentation
- Train models on many-GPU clusters, including debugging instability, throughput bottlenecks, and failed runs
- Support periodic retraining and iterative model improvement
...
Evaluation & Quality
...
- Develop and maintain benchmarking and evaluation workflows that track model quality, runtime, and cost
- Validate on external benchmarks while prioritising internal discovery performance
...
Engineering & Collaboration
...
- Write high-quality research and engineering code: refactor, package, test, and document ML components to support team velocity
- Collaborate with ML and software colleagues to enable model deployment
- Mentor interns through technical guidance, code reviews, and best practices in ML experimentation
...
- PhD in ML, Computer Science, or a computational STEM discipline - or equivalent industry experience demonstrating comparable depth
...
- Strong Python and PyTorch experience, including end-to-end implementation and training of deep learning models
...
- Demonstrated experience applying deep learning to 3D or physical-science problems (e.g. structural biology, protein modelling, computational chemistry, scientific
...
- ML)Strong engineering habits: reproducible experimentation, clean code, testing, and performance-minded debugging
...
- Comfort working with modern ML infrastructure (e.g. Docker, CUDA, Kubernetes, experiment tracking tools)
...
- Generative modelling: diffusion models, flow matching, stochastic interpolants, or related frameworks
...
- Protein-ligand modelling, structure prediction, or structure-based drug discovery (SBDD) workflows
...
- Geometric deep learning and/or equivariant neural network architectures
...
- Multi-GPU and distributed training at scale
...
- HPC or large-scale training operations
...

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

Hello, I am Chandan Kumar a Principle Data Scientist with Executive Certification in Data Science. Areas of interest are GenAI, Machine learning, Neural Networks and Artificial Intelligence. Around 14 years of extensive experience in GenAI, Statistical Modeling, Forecasting/Predictive Analytics, Segmentation Methodologies, Regression and Classification Based Models to deliver insights and implement action-oriented solutions to complex business problems.  Responsible for setting up the Data Science Capabilities within the organization.  Provide strategic direction and leadership in the development and execution of data science and machine learning initiatives aligned with the organization's goals and objectives.  Skilled in leading and executing the development of large-scale distributed applications utilizing micro services architecture, leveraging expertise in Python/Java, machine learning, cloud computing, and big data technologies.  Designing and developing GenAI based solution using Azure OpenAI-GPT4, Used Vector Database, Langchain and other GenAI's technology and capabilities.  Utilising Azure data storage solutions like Azure Blob Storage, Azure SQL Database, and Azure Cosmos DB to manage data used for training and inference.  Good exposure to build a Data pipeline (Data Preparation, Transformation, Scaling and Feature Engineering)  Hands on experience in building Prediction models using Linear regression, Logistic regression, Decision Tree, K-Means, PCA, Random Forest & KNN etc.  Test design to roll out new product features & measuring success using significance statistics.  Worked on many projects using NLP with other ML algorithms.  Worked with other tools such as Azure, DataBricks, Azure Data Factory etc.  Application deployment using CI/CD, Containers, Kubernetes (Orchestration),DevOps.  Effective communicator with the ability to collaborate cross-functionally with stakeholders from different backgrounds, translate technical concepts into actionable insights, and drive data-driven decision making

Working on an Agentic AI and multi‑modal RAG systems, designing LLM‑powered workflows that can plan, call tools, and collaborate as autonomous agents to solve complex business problems. Build retrieval‑augmented generation pipelines over text, code, and multi‑modal data (PDFs, tables, Images, APIs), using vector databases and evaluation frameworks to ensure reliability, latency, and cost‑efficiency. Recent work includes: Design and deploy Agentic AI systems for healthcare, powering autonomous agents that orchestrate multi-step clinical workflows—planning, tool-calling, and multi-agent collaboration over hcp records Implementing production RAG (chunking, re‑ranking, query‑rewriting, guardrails, feedback loops) for domain‑specific copilots. Integrating LLMs into existing products via REST/GRPC, queues, and event‑driven pipelines, with monitoring, tracing, and A/B evaluation. Interested in roles where deep LLM reasoning, agentic workflows, and multi‑modal retrieval can directly move product and revenue metrics. Engineered agentic workflows where LLM agents dynamically route tasks—chunk clinical docs/images, retrieve via RAG, critique outputs, and invoke APIs for real-time treatment recommendations. ​ Implemented multi-modal RAG end-to-end: Advanced chunking (recursive+semantic) → multi-encoder embeddings → Azure Vector store → dense/sparse retrieval → LLM synthesis with scoring. Optimized retrieval accuracy via re-rankers (ColBERT/cross-encoders) and query routers, processing. Deployed production pipelines with observability (LangSmith/tracing), A/B testing, and domain-specific fine-tuning.
